name: Korean Catholic
description_short: Korean Martyrs Catholic Center is a Korean-language Catholic parish in Westminster, CA, offering spiritual services, sacraments, faith formation, and community ministries since 1977.
description_long:
Korean Martyrs Catholic Center (KMCC) was officially established on September 22, 1977, as the first formally recognized Korean Catholic community in the Orange County Diocese of California. It was founded by a small group of Korean Catholic immigrants who gathered to discuss the need for Korean-language Mass services. The parish is located in Westminster, CA, and has served as a foundational community for Korean Catholicism in Orange County, leading to the establishment of other Korean Catholic parishes including St. Thomas Korean Community and St. Elizabeth Korean Community.

KMCC offers a comprehensive range of Catholic sacramental and spiritual services including baptism (infant and adult), confirmation, first communion, marriage, anointing of the sick, and funeral rites. The parish operates Christian Faith Formation (CFF) programs for children from pre-K through junior high school, adult religious education through the Rite of Christian Initiation for Adults (RCIA), and numerous faith-based ministries including prayer groups, Bible study circles, Rosary Army, young adult programming (IGNITE), and service organizations like the St. Vincent de Paul Society. The parish also offers Eucharistic adoration, Zoom-based spiritual events, and community counseling resources.

KMCC distinguishes itself as a historic Korean-language parish with deep roots in the Korean Catholic immigrant community and a commitment to bilingual (English/Korean) ministry. The parish maintains numerous lay organizations and ministries specifically designed for different life stages and spiritual interests. Under the pastoral leadership of Father Eugene and previous Columban priests, the community has developed extensive faith formation infrastructure including dedicated classroom resources and youth programming. The parish is formally affiliated with the Roman Catholic Diocese of Orange and explicitly commits to child safety protocols.

As a religious organization, KMCC is not a consumer finance provider and was incorrectly categorized as a payday-alternative lender. The parish has no lending products, financial services, or credit-related offerings. It is purely a Catholic faith community providing sacramental ministry, spiritual guidance, religious education, and community support services to members.
services: Catholic sacraments (baptism, confirmation, first communion, marriage, anointing of sick)
Christian Faith Formation (CFF) programs for children pre-K through junior high
Rite of Christian Initiation for Adults (RCIA) - adult conversion education
Eucharistic adoration and Eucharistic visitation services
Rosary Army and prayer group ministries
Young adult programming (IGNITE praise and adoration)
St. Vincent de Paul Society charitable outreach
Lay ministry organizations (various sodalities and societies)
pros: Offers bilingual (English/Korean) Catholic services meeting specific cultural and linguistic needs of Korean immigrants
Comprehensive sacramental coverage including baptism, confirmation, marriage, anointing of sick, and funeral services
Established faith formation programs for children (CFF) from pre-K through junior high school
Adult religious education through RCIA for those seeking to enter the Catholic faith
Extensive lay ministries and organizations for different demographics (youth, young adults, families, seniors)
Eucharistic adoration and online Zoom spiritual events providing flexible access
Affiliated with Roman Catholic Diocese of Orange with formal child safety commitments
cons: No financial services or lending products offered despite payday-alternative categorization
Website navigation is complex with dual English/Korean menus and outdated content (references to 2020-2023 events)
Limited online accessibility or modern digital services beyond Zoom events
No consumer finance resources, credit counseling, or financial assistance programs available
Parish-specific services with no broader community or non-member accessibility
best_for: Korean-speaking Catholics seeking culturally appropriate faith community and sacramental services
Korean immigrant families wanting bilingual religious education for children
Adults exploring Catholicism through structured faith formation and RCIA programs
diagnosis: Korean Martyrs Catholic Center is a religious parish, not a consumer finance company, and should not be listed on CreditDoc. It offers exclusively spiritual, sacramental, and faith formation services to Korean Catholic community members. No financial products, lending services, credit tools, or consumer finance assistance are available.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
